entry.title: Ratings on Compensation Worksheet appearing in Decimals (Standard Integration of PM & Comp)
entry.content:
Hi All,

Our Compensation form pulls ratings from the performance forms, However the Performance rating is displayed in decimals without label on the compensation worksheets, but only for a few users. The rating scale applied in the performance form section(Competency) which we want to pull and in Compensation rating source setting is same. The type is set to default in Compensation. We had the same issue last year but it was automatically solved so we could find the root cause as we couldn't replicate it and this year its the same. Has anybody faced this issue before.

Kind Note: We are not using Matrix grid scale anywhere in PM.

@xavierlegarrec  Would be great if you share your thoughts or experience on the same in case you have also faced this issue in the past or have seen customers raising this.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: BAPI_PO_CREATE1: Create Service Purchase Orders with Multiple Lines
entry.content:
Nice Blog

Good example.

 

Thank you

entry.title: Cannot add Search Help to Custom Field - "No suitable value help key field exists" error
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I made a Custom Field in the customizing client of our dev system (S/4HANA Public Cloud - 3 system landscape) through the Custom Fields app. I have a requirement to add search help to this custom field. For this purpose, I made a custom CDS view through the Custom CDS Views app. However, when I try to bind this CDS through the "Value Help View" field, the error message "No suitable value help key field exists" appears:

 

Here is the setup of my custom CDS view:

What am I missing? Any help is welcome. Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Mass changes to purchasing info record
entry.content:
Hello Experts ,

 

while doing mass changes in info record we got error while in application job 

error message :-INSERT A MEDICATION IN THE COMPONENT MM-PUR-GF-MAS

What does it means any authorization missing or anything else on this ...?

entry.title: Architecting S/4HANA Extensibility: Full Stack Extensions with Clean Core Paradigm
entry.content:
Introduction

In the ever evolving landscape of enterprise applications, designing and developing a scalable and secure business applications is a crucial skill for developers, consultants and architects. This blog showcases a full stack project that brings together SAP and open source technologies to deliver a robust solution.

I have tried to showcase the important components of the project and how they are translated to executing blocks once deployed on cloud. The blog also touches cloud (Business Technology Platform) services which are important in many customer use cases. 

The intent of this blog is to provide you the starting point and give you a leverage to explore possibilities on your own and ofcourse, with the help of your preferred co-pilot!

At the end of the blog, I have also provided my GitHub repo where you can find a complete project for your reference.

I will breakdown the article into 3 parts

  1. Use Case
  2. Solution architecture
  3. Components of the architecture

Use Case

I have developed a full stack application adhering to the architecture and developed based on a use case. It is available on GitHub.

The README file takes you through each and every step starting from use case till full fledge enterprise solution.

The project is more than just a standalone application. It serves as a blueprint for SAP extensibility while adhering to the Clean Core strategy. By leveraging SAP BTP services, CAP, Fiori, and seamless S/4HANA integration, this approach ensures that businesses can extend their ERP capabilities without modifications to the core system.

This architecture is highly adaptable and can be referred to your specific use case.

Solution Architecture

Below diagram provides the architecture of the solution. It also shows the transition from design time to run time artifacts and how the components fit along with seamless integration with the BTP services.

Figure-1

Components of the architecture

In this section, let us go through all the components shown in the above diagram and understand the details around each one of it and how these components are translated to the code for the specific use case.

The complete solution is built on the robust SAP Business Technology Platform.

Development/Design Time

I have extracted a part of the architecture diagram below to focus on the design/development aspects:

Figure-2

Developer develops the application on Business Application Studio. When creating a full-stack CAP application, there are 6 important components which are explained below to showcase some critical files inside each of the directories.

  1. app - This directory contains all the UI content. This means, when you create UI apps (standard or freestyle FIORI), all the UI content for each app goes inside this folder. This is the face of your application.
    1. Take a look at the content of one of the UI apps - sales. You will see webapp folder which contains the UI code adhering to the MVC pattern. For standard FIORI you won't see much UI code in webapp as the UI is rendered using FIORI AppComponent using the OData annotations. Take a look at the freestyle FIORI app - commissionconfig. Here you will see controllers, views and stylesheets.
    2. The second important component inside the UI app is the xs-app.json. This is important as this is used by the application router to define routes/destinations and provide authentication type.
  2. db: This is the foundation of your application/project. Here you define the entity model and the relationships between the entities. Take a look at the schema.cds. This folder also holds the initial data to be loaded inside the data folder. 
  3. srv: This is the heart of your application. This directory holds the services and event handler where you can add annotations, custom logic and secure your entities. This folder also holds external API service entities. 
  4. xs-security.json: Securing your application is as important as developing it. XSUAA service in BTP is leveraged for this. It gives role based access to the resources of your application. Take a look at the file: xs-security.json. Here you will see role templates and scopes defined which are translated to your XSUAA instance at runtime.
  5. package.json: This is one of the important file which packages your project by defining the dependencies, start up scripts and most important cds configurations for development and production environments. Browse through this file: package.json.
  6. mta.yaml: This is the deployment descriptor and orchestrator. In this file you define the modules of your application, resources required by your application and dependencies. This file is used during the deployment of the project to cloud foundry environment. During deployment, as per the configuration provided in this file, service instances will be created and application will be deployed to the environment accordingly. Do not get overwhelmed by the structure of the file, when you start building the application, you will understand. Take a quick look at the file: mta.yaml. Refer the mta build tool help docs here - https://sap.github.io/cloud-mta-build-tool/

Deployment/Run Time:

Now, let us see how the above components translate after deployment. I have extracted a part of the architecture diagram below to focus on the runtime/deployment aspects:

Figure-3

When you deploy the project, using the multi target application descriptor file, the MTA deployer creates the resources defined in the resources section of the mta.yaml. Let's take an example of the HDI container and XSUAA service instance required by the application runtime i.e. srv. In the below fragment, you will see the module sales-commission-srv and dependencies in the requires section

If you go down in the resources section, you will see the dependencies for HDI container and XSUAA service.

  • If you observe the XSUAA resource, you will see it takes the xs-security.json during deployment and creates the scopes at the deployment time.
  • Once the HDI container is created, the entities described in schema.cds are created in the database container.

Now, let's come to the UI part and see how the artifacts are translated from design time to runtime during deployment

As you know, HTML5 repository provides a central place to store the static content of the HTML5 applications. Deep dive on HTML5 repository can be found here.

So, to add the UI content to the HTML5 repository on BTP, you have to add the service in your mta.yaml as shown in the below fragment

 Once you update the HTML5 repository, it will pull the UI content from your project.

Destination Service: The destination service is required to store the API destinations which can be used at runtime to connect to the backend APIs. In our project, we have a use case to connect to the backend API_BUSINESS_PARTNER. In package.json you will see the API_BUSINESS_PARTNER configuration.

You create the destination - BP_DESTINATION in the destination service which will be used by the application when connecting to the backend API.

The Application Router & SAP Build Work Zone: The last two critical pieces in the puzzle. These are the gateway to your application.

  • Application Router: The application router is the single point of entry for your application. This is the secured gateway to your application resources. You can leverage SAP managed application router or even extend the application router by installing it manually. At runtime, the application router fetches the xs-app.json from the UI content and uses it for routing and authentication leveraging XSUAA service. 
  • SAP Build Work Zone: This is the launch pad where you can launch your applications, group your applications and also hide/show applications based on roles. The managed application router is provided with SAP Build Work Zone subscription.

Conclusion

Drawing from my experience, I must emphasize that these insights can serve as a starting point, though their applicability may vary based on specific scenarios. Also there will be most probably customizations required as per your specific use case.

In summary, SAP BTP provides robust ecosystem that empowers enterprises with flexible integration, secure connectivity and efficient development. 

This project will surely give you a holistic view from design and development of cloud native application and eventually deployment on cloud in production scenarios.

It's never too late to start  —  GitHub Repo

Stay curious! Keep learning!

Feel free to “like“, “Share“, “Add a Comment” and to get more updates about my next blogs follow me!

References

  1. https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/get-started/
  2. https://developers.sap.com/
  3. https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/topic/851bde42e4e1410c96abbe402fa9128c 
  4. https://api.sap.com/api/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER
  5. https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-standard-edition/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition/what-is-sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition
  6. https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/application-router

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud infrastructure is moving to SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @Westmoreland,

are there any updates on the timeline for the HANA Cloud migration?

Kind regards,
Sebastian

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud Decrypt last 4 digits of credit card (Concur Financial Posting)
entry.content:
How do we find or decrypt the last 4 digits of the credit card used for the expense report posted in S/4 public cloud? Right now,  it is showing an encrypted characters.

We need to decrypt it to create remittance advice. 

Process:

1. Employee submits an expense report in Concur

2. It gets approved in Concur

3. It goes to S/4 public cloud and get posted

4. In the document in S/4, item text shows encrypted codes and does not show any of the last 4 digits of the card that we can use to create a remittance advice. 

Anyone here could help us find a way to get those last 4 digits, please?

entry.title: Why numbers in dynamic launchpad tiles in latest sapui5 versions are not shown?
entry.content: Hi, SAP Learning! What must be learned to understand why numbers in launchpad dynamic tiles were shown in sapui5 version 1.95.0 and are not shown in sapui5 version 1.108.31?

entry.title: Re: SAC for Planning - DATEDIFF with variable-member or i...
entry.content:
Hello @Deb_D 

I'm no real Data Actions expert... but checking the SAC Help page I'm wondering a bit that your variables start with # instead are %Income% and %Outcome% ?

Example there: @DiffMonth = DATEDIFF(%EndDate%, TODAY(), "MONTH")

Maybe it helps? Otherwise you really need an expert here 😅

BR, Martin

entry.title: Hello Everyone,I want to add a tab in the additional data in CV03N Tcode, do we have any BADI
entry.content: I want to add a tab in additional tab in CV03N tcode i tried to add in user exit - CV110001

entry.title: Re: Mass changes to purchasing info record
entry.content:
I would have expected an error message such as Please create an incident for component MM-PUR-GF-MAS?

(What was the exact error message, I have a doubt about this capitalised message, are you usin a SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management system?)

entry.title: Re: Rewards & Recognition: Guidelines formula
entry.content:
Hi Anna - I think the best option there would be to create a pay component labelled percent/percentage and link this to the program. Then within the rule, set the award currency code to equal the currency code which is within compInformation. Something like this

Thanks,

Andres

entry.title: Update the dimension member in a transactional record in model
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a requirement to update the dimension member of a particular row in the transactional data of a model.

For example, i have below transactional data in a model:

Dim ADim BDim CAmount
#BT01DI11112345
#BT02DI11134567

And i want update record like this:

Dim ADim BDim CAmount
MD01BT01DI11112345
MD01BT02DI11134567

Is it possible to achieve this?

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SLT: Add field to target structure from logging table
entry.content:
Hi Ian,

this sounded good, but I do not have a _it_io_s_logtab table in my BoR rule for table BUT000.
I only have table _IT_IO_S_BUT000 with field /1DH/OPERATION and not the IUUC_TIMESTAMP.
I use embedded SLT in S/4 HANA 2023 FPS2.
What else can I do?

Thanks and regards
Jens

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to display Custom Application Logs in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
Hi @Danii

The only way I found for the users to access the logs was to create a Custom Entity with a custom class that reads the logs using class CL_BALI_LOG_DB. I added a couple of annotations to the custom entity and created a UI Service Binding. Unfortunately, I couldn't test further since my client doesn't want to pay for Business Application Studio, because he says this should be a functionality included by SAP. And I agree with him.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Task Center: Innovations and Updates
entry.content:
SAP Task Center revolutionizes how organizations manage approval workflows by consolidating tasks from various SAP systems into a single, intuitive inbox. This centralized solution is designed to streamline operational processes and reduce administrative overhead. It empowers users to swiftly handle approvals across multiple applications, whether on the web or via mobile devices. By unifying diverse approval items into one accessible list, SAP Task Center enhances efficiency and consistency, and minimizes implementation costs. It is the ideal tool for businesses seeking a seamless, cross-platform experience. 

In this blog post, we'll dive into the latest enhancements in SAP Task Center. We're relentlessly refining the solution to ensure it stays ahead of the curve, and I'm excited to share the newest features and updates that further boost productivity and streamline your approval processes. Stay tuned as we explore the cutting-edge innovations designed to enhance your experience and drive operational excellence. 

SAP Task Center is now included with SAP Build Work Zone 

Starting January 2025, SAP Task Center is included in all editions of SAP Build Work Zone, extending its capabilities for our customers. This inclusion applies to SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition (both Subscription and Pay-Per-Use), SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition (Pay-Per-Use), and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone (Subscription).  

SAP Task Center in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

SAP Task Center in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition 

Each licensed user is entitled to 20 tasks, aggregated at the Global Account level and shareable across users. For customers requiring additional tasks, SAP Task Center offers an extended licensing option via BTPEA.  To take full advantage of this new commercial model, customers are required to use the updated Task Center service plans. The Cloud-only plan is designed exclusively for SAP Start and is limited to cloud tasks. All-tasks plan is available in conjunction with SAP Build Work Zone editions, supports cloud, on-premise, and third-party tasks. Existing customers on the previous Standard and On-Premise and Third-Party service plans can continue with their current plans until contract renewals, after which a deprecation plan will be introduced. 

For the setup process check out this Discovery Center mission “Establish a central inbox with SAP Task Center”.

SAP Task Center Availability for Partners' Licenses 

SAP Task Center is now also included with all editions of SAP Build Work Zone for partners’ licenses. Similar as above, it applies to SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition (both Subscription and Pay-Per-Use), SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition (Pay-Per-Use), and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone (Subscription).  

With the availability of the new service plans as part of the SAP Build Work Zone TDD licenses, partners can integrate SAP Task Center into their own environments for testing and development purposes. This inclusion enables them to showcase how centralized task management can streamline approvals and enhance workflow efficiency within SAP solutions, ultimately helping their customers improve productivity and collaboration.     

 Real User Monitoring for SAP Task Center 

 SAP Task Center now integrates with SAP Cloud ALM (Application Lifecycle Management) to deliver robust real user monitoring, empowering administrators with detailed insights into application usage and performance. This integration tracks all inbound and outbound communication events through the SAP Task Center REST API, capturing critical task management events that are essential for troubleshooting.  

 By monitoring both user interactions and server requests, the Real User Monitoring tool offers comprehensive visualizations, enabling proactive management of system performance and swift resolution of issues. 

Read more about this on SAP help portal. 

*****

I hope you enjoyed this blogpost. If you want to learn more, feel free to explore the additional resources listed below. 

➤ SAP Task Center on SAP Community  

➤ SAP Build Work Zone SAP Community  

➤ What’s New for SAP Task Center

➤ SAP Task Center FAQs

➤ Getting Started with SAP Task Center on SAP BTP Blog post

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Challenge March - SAP Community Escape House - AI 🤖🧠
entry.content: Challenge completed! Thank you for the effort put into the very elaborate format, which made it fun and attractive. I would have liked a bit more difficulty and more open-ended questions where I could have been more creative.

entry.title: LTMC for Open Items in FI-CA (PSCD, FPE1) – Missing Migration Object?
entry.content:
Hello everyone

I want to use LTMC to migrate open items in FI-CA (PSCD, FPE1). However, I cannot find a suitable migration object for this scenario.

My questions:

  1. Is there a predefined migration object for open items in FI-CA?
  2. If not, what would be the best approach? Should I create a custom migration object using LTMC or would LSMW be the better alternative?
  3. Are there any best practices or experiences regarding this specific use case?

Both approaches are new to me, so I would greatly appreciate any guidance or insights.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: BAdI SD_SLS_MODIFY_HEAD is not called as expected when processing an Invoice
entry.content:
1. We have created a new custom field and are filling it through a custom logic via SD_SLS_MODIFY_HEAD, but the new value is not transferred.
  2. We create sales order 123 on our system. We later adjusted the logic in BAdI SD_SLS_MODIFY_HEAD to fill a custom field with text "TEST". When we create/process the billing document for sales order 123 we expect to see this custom field filled with the text "TEST" as per the new logic.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BAdI SD_SLS_MODIFY_HEAD is not called as expected whe...
entry.content:
Please note that the BAdI SD_SLS_MODIFY_HEAD is used for sales orders. It is not called during invoice processing. Before creating the invoice, please try making a relevant change to the sales order or creating a new sales order - the BAdI will be called as expected. 

If you are ever unsure if your BAdI is getting called, please consider using the Custom Logic Trace. See link below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/4161479993824dde8f088419d8730833.html

It maybe another option for you to consider using Data transfer BAdI:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/3714184b72b54dad819a7c398a701152.html

entry.title: Re: Handle translations on custom codelist
entry.content:
By using the "Virtaal" application, it's easier to modify the XLIFF files.

It's adding the needed xml nodes so the modifications are detected by SAP Cloud Applications Studio.

The activation of the translations still need a dummy modification in the corresponding codelist: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2926215

entry.title: Re: how to create and expose OData APIs
entry.content:
Hi DaniEmmenegger,
Thanks for your comment , For your answer please refer https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/odata-vs-api-mangement-difference/qaq-p/504119

entry.title: SuccessFactors-Problem hiring employees through "Manage Pending Hires"
entry.content:
Around date 1/12/2025 we had a problem with hiring employees in the SuccessFactors system via the standard 'Hiring Pending Management,' as it was creating a serious problem for us in hiring employees; the recruiters were hiring employees through an alternative way, which was creating problems for us in terms of converting a candidate into an employee.

The problem is now fixed (unfraternally we don't know how it is fixed), but more than anything, we wanted to check what the cause of the problem was and if there is a correct way to prevent these problems in the future.

entry.title: Re: The Candidate mobile number disappeared from ADR2 table
entry.content:
I am over a decade late. But I came across this question, when i was searching for solution, where BUT000-ADDRCOMM was empty, when BUPA_CENTRAL_GET_DETAIL was trying to retrieve the email ID of a candidate in E-Recruitment system, which led to some issues in our process.

In my case, BUT000-ADDRCOMM got filled when in t-code BP, Tab 'Address', 'Address Independent Communication' section was filled with email-id.

entry.title: Re: Fiori App 'Manage Documents' - Object link data not configured
entry.content: We are still facing the same issue ,

entry.title: Applying VAT Split in Payments in Contract Accounting
entry.content:
In this blog post we introduce the split payment of VAT mechanism in Contract Accounting (FI-CA). The VAT split in this blog post means that during the payment run, the information of VAT amounts is split from the invoice amounts in the outgoing payment file. That is, there is only one payment, together with the VAT and invoice amount.

Note: If you want to split the VAT payment and the invoice payment and post VAT amount on a separate bank clearing account (In other words, there are two payments in total), you must set up the bank clearing account for posting area 1061. (Transaction: FQC0)

By applying the VAT split function, the VAT-relevant payment becomes more visible and enables the house banks to send this VAT amount directly to the tax authority. This feature improves the transparency and efficiency of processing payments.

Business Scenario

When you apply the VAT split feature, the VAT amount is listed separately in the payment file. When the house bank receives the payment file, it pays the VAT amount directly to the tax authority.

Processing outgoing with VAT split follows the standard payment processing as the picture above.

  1. You first create and post contract accounting documents.
  2. You execute payment runs. In this step the system carries out several actions, including selecting payment methods and bank details that you have defined in the customizing activity.
  3. After the payment runs, a payment file is created. You can download the payment file as a .txt, or .xml file based on the payment format that you have assigned to payment methods for outgoing payments.
    The VAT split is carried out in this step when the payment file is generated. The VAT amount is listed separately. See the example and the line marked in yellow below.

  4. You send the payment file to the payee’s house bank.
  5. Payee’s house bank then sends the VAT amount directly to the tax authority. Later on, it sends you back a statement with the payment status.
  6. Finally, you upload the received bank statement file.

Prerequisites

Before processing the payment with the VAT split feature, you must complete the configuration steps. In this blog post we introduce the configuration in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

The following two activities are standard steps for processing payments.

  1. Define Payment Methods (ID: 101952): Define one or more country/region payment methods for outgoing payments.
  2. Define Specifications for Paying Company Code (ID: 101251): Select company code used for automatic payment transactions and assign the payment methods.

To apply the VAT split for processing payments, you must complete the settings as follows. In the following print screen, we take Poland as an example.

  1. Define Payment Methods for VAT Split in Contract Accounting (ID: 106940): Assign a payment method to the country/region key for VAT split.
    • Enter the country/region key and select Apply.
    • Select a payment method and save.
  2. Business partner master data setting:
    • Search for the Maintain Business Partner app.
    • Enter the business partner.
    • In the Display in BP Role, select Contract Partner.
    • In the Country-Specific Data (FI-CA), enter a valid time for the VAT split and select 2-Split Enabled as the VAT Payment Type.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

The following two activities are standards steps for processing payments.

  1. Define Payment Methods (Transaction: FQP1): Define one or more country/region payment methods for outgoing payments.
  2. Define Specifications for Paying Company Code (Transaction: FQP4): Select company code used for automatic payment transactions and assign the payment methods.

To apply the VAT split for processing payments, you must complete the settings as follows. In the following print screen, we take Poland as an example.

  1. Define Payment Methods for VAT Split in Contract Accounting: In the transaction SM30 enter the view FKKID_VATSP_PM_V to assign a payment method to the country/region key for VAT split.
  2. Enable the VAT Split Option Screen (Transaction BUS6):
    • In the Screen sequences, select the entry BUP001.

    • In the Screen sequences – Screens, enter a screen code and an item number.
      Take Poland sample values as example. In the next setting, you enter events to this item number.

      Item

      Screen

      9000005

      MKKPL1

  3. Enter Business Data Toolset Events (Transaction: BUS7)
    • Select for the events based on your business requirements.
    • In the Event -> Function Modules enter the item number and the function module.

    • You can refer to the table below to add the item into the events according to your business requirements.

      Event

      Item

      Function Module

      Call

      Application

      DCHCK

      9000005

      FKKPL_BUPA_VATSP_EVENT_DCHCK

      X

      MKK

      DLVE1

      9000005

      FKKPL_BUPA_VATSP_EVENT_DLVE1

      X

      MKK

      DSAVC

      9000005

      FKKPL_BUPA_VATSP_EVENT_DSAVC

      X

      MKK

      DSAVE

      9000005

      FKKPL_BUPA_VATSP_EVENT_DSAVE

      X

      MKK

      ISDAT

      9000005

      FKKPL_BUPA_VATSP_EVENT_ISDAT

      X

      MKK

      ISSTA

      9000005

      FKKPL_BUPA_VATSP_EVENT_ISSTA

      X

      MKK

      XCHNG

      9000005

      FKKPL_BUPA_VATSP_EVENT_XCHNG

      X

      MKK

       

  4. Business partner master data setting (Transaction: BP)
    • Enter the business partner.
    • In the Display in BP Role, select Contract Partner.
    • In the Country-Specific Data (FI-CA), enter a valid time for the VAT split and select 2-Split Enabled as the VAT Payment Type.

SAP Notes

You can refer to the SAP Notes for VAT Splits for localization in Poland.

  1. 2653460: VAT split payment format PL_ELIXIR-O and maintenance for MKK partner
  2. 2664940: VAT split payment format PL_ELIXIR-O and maintenance for MKK partner, correction #1

Is this blog post useful to you? Leave your comments to let us know your thoughts!

entry.title: IBP RTI: Queue is Locked
entry.content:
We have installed all known applicable notes to enable RTI from our ECC system to IBP, and have configured everything in ECC and IBP per the help documentation. Data seems to flow after activating the model in ECC, and the data shows in the "Monitor bgRFC Queues" app within IBP, but the the queues show with a queue status of "Queue is locked". We can process records by starting the queue within the app, but this only processes a single unit, then goes to status "Queue is locked" again. We have to do this over and over for every unit of data. 

Are we missing something to automatically trigger the starting of the bgRFC queues?

entry.title: Re: Hello Everyone,I want to add a tab in the additional data in CV03N Tcode, do we have any BADI
entry.content: What is the problem specifically? What have you tried?

entry.title: Re: Create TO by Posting Change Delivery - NO BDC or FUNC
entry.content:
Hello,

Could you please share your solution? I am encountering the same error with Posting Change Delivery.

entry.title: Re: Setup and usage of contract accounts
entry.content:
Hi Henk:

Of course the system can accommodate many designs here.  It depends on how you want to process the receivables for these SD charges and who processes them.  In NA, most utilities want a single invoice inclusive of all charges both commodity and non-commodity, so a single contract account used for both processes is the norm.  If you need/want to keep them separate, then a distinct CA type makes sense and can enable you to filter the account selection in SD either manually or in an automated fashion.  As a general rule, more master data makes it more challenging for users so i would always look to minimize that while still enabling the processes as necessary.

regards,

bill.

entry.title: What's New in SAP Convergent Charging 2023 FPS03
entry.content:
New Release

PUBLIC

 

February 2025

Dear Convergent Charging and BRIM community,

We are glad to announce another release of the latest SAP Convergent Charging 2023 FPS3, which introduces intuitive features designed to enhance functionality and user experience. This update brings exciting capabilities, including new monitoring metrics for business data growth, streamlined cockpit deployment via SAPInst, enhanced Fiori App and integration capabilities, and an improved, more user-friendly cockpit experience.

Let's dive into these latest product features in detail, highlighting our latest pricing and charging capabilities as part of the SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (BRIM) suite.

New metrics for monitoring business data growth

Managing business data growth is critical for ensuring optimal system performance. The latest enhancement in this release introduces proactive monitoring metrics to track key entities such as Subscriber Account Count, Contract Count, and Mapping Table Row Count. By continuously monitoring data growth, you can identify potential issues early, rather than reacting when performance problems have already occurred. This ensures better system stability and smooth operations, even as solutions evolve and new implementations drive data increases.

With this feature, customers can gain greater control and predictability over the system landscape. With the new standard view that shows the evolution of the growth in the row of the key metrics rows over the months helps detect anomalies before they impact performance, while historical data tracking enables more accurate forecasting and capacity planning. This not only helps prevent unexpected slowdowns but also improves operational efficiency, ensuring a seamless experience with an uninterrupted charging process.

Key Benefits:

  • Track Growth of Business Data Objects – Gain real-time insights into expanding business data, helping identify trends and prevent performance bottlenecks.
  • Improve User Assistance and Support – Enhance system monitoring, streamline troubleshooting, and provide proactive support to maintain high availability and reliability.

New Introscope metrics are now available for effective monitoring, allowing users to define thresholds and set alerts for these metrics. For more information about this new feature, refer to the Display Usage Metrics topic and details regarding Introscope on the SAP Help Portal.

Facilitation of the deployment of the Cockpit with an installer

With this release, the deployment of SAP CC Cockpit has been simplified by integrating it with SAPInst, aligning its installation process with the Core Server. This ensures a more structured and streamlined setup, reducing complexity and making it easier for businesses to adopt the Cockpit. With this improvement, the , ensuring a more cohesive deployment process within the SAP CC system.

By integrating Cockpit deployment into SAPInst, businesses can experience improved system stability and reduced support incidents related to manual deployment. This new method provides of deployment provides more standardized and efficient approach to installation and gain higher adoption of Cockpit. While the existing option of deploying on Apache Tomcat remains available. 

Feature enablement

  • Simplify the deployment of the Cockpit: Easily install and update the Cockpit through SAPInst, reducing manual efforts.
  • Minimizes errors and deployment issues and provides flexibility
  • Reduce the number of support incidents linked to the deployment of the CC Cockpit

Learn more about this feature from our SAP help page for Installation and Maintenance Guide

Enhancement of the Display Contracts Fiori app to display allowances of a contract

With this release, the Display Contracts Fiori app in Cockpit has been further enhanced to provide a display of associated allowances assigned in a contract. This update enables users to quickly locate and review contract details, including associated contract and its activated allowances, ensuring greater transparency and accuracy in rating and billing processes. With this enhancement, billing specialists can efficiently verify provisioned provider contract data, along with key parameters and counters of the assigned allowances, thus improving overall operational efficiency.

Key Benefits:

  • Enhanced Contract Visibility – Easily access and review allowances linked to a contract, ensuring transparency in charging.
  • Improved Data Verification – view the activated allowance logic of an allowance including activation date/time & current value of counters.
  • Increased Operational Efficiency – Enable billing specialists to quickly verify contract details, enhancing workflow efficiency and accuracy.

Learn more about this feature from SAP Help portal:     Cockpit Apps > Display Contracts

Other Enhancements 

In addition to the features mentioned above, SAP Convergent Charging 2023 FPS03 also introduces other enhancements:

  • SAP Note for Minimum Hardware Requirements for an SAP Convergent Charging Development Environment

Our customer has requested an optimal hardware configuration for running a development environment. In response, we have released SAP Note 3568851, which outlines the essential hardware requirements for creating and testing pricing configurations.

By following these guidelines, customers can significantly reduce infrastructure costs by avoiding oversized hardware configurations. Adhering to these recommendations not only ensures optimal performance but also enhances overall operational efficiency. We strongly encourage our customers to implement these best practices for a cost-effective and high-performing development environment.

Find more details on the hardware configuration required, refer to the SAP Note: 3568851

 

  • Support for additional pricing components in the Cockpit

The latest update introduces two powerful pricing components to enhance flexibility and precision in charge calculations. The "Number Matching" comparator (formerly Interval Switching) allows the pricing specialists to compare numeric properties against specific values or intervals, ensuring more granular control over pricing rules.

Additionally, the "Number Split-Based Addition" aggregator (formerly Numbers Splitter) enables more dynamic pricing in the pricing tree of the charges and in pricing functions.

     Benefits

  • Enhance support for more complex pricing scenarios from the Cockpit

Find more details about this feature from the SAP Help portal:

  • Comparators > Number Matching
  • Aggregators > Number Split-Based Addition

 

  • Renaming of nodes in pricing trees

To improve usability, we’ve introduced a Rename action in the Manage Rate Plans and Manage Pricing Functions apps. Available in both the toolbar and context menu, this feature allows users to rename pricing tree nodes for better business relevance or can even reset node name to the default name computed by the app. Additionally, tooltips now display component names for enhanced clarity. These improvements streamline pricing configuration, making it more intuitive and user-friendly.

Find more detail about this feature from SAP Help portal:

  • Configuring the Pricing Tree of a Charge > Renaming a Node
  • Configuring the Pricing Tree of a Pricing Function > Renaming a Node

 

  • 3rd party updates

With this release, JCo has been upgraded to version 3.1, ensuring improved connectivity and performance. Additionally, we’ve introduced an optional check for JCo destination in SAPInst, enhancing installation flexibility.

More Information

SAP Convergent Charging 2023 FPS 03 has been released and is generally available to customers from Feb 2025 and is available on the SAP Software Download Center.

For more information on SAP Convergent Charging 2023 FPS 03, check out the following links:

  • What’s New in 2023 FPS03
  • Delivery and Information Note for SAP Convergent Charging 2023 FPS 3
  • Product Page on SAP Help Portal   > Convergent Charging
  • Questions and Answers for SAP Convergent Charging in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP S/4 Public cloud - Service - ATP check on service quotation
entry.content:
Dear Experts, 

I have a question : I made workshop with my customer in order to implement S/4 public cloud Service module. In the SAP training course S4C25, in the unit 4GA for service quotation, lesson "Explaining the Service Quotation Process Steps", a paragraph explains the ATP logic. It sounds good for my customer requirement. Great !

When I check in the system : 

1. I can't find the service quotation item category in CBC in order to activate ATP check, I can only see service order item category. And I'm not able to add new entries... I am in the customizing activity mentionned by the training course.

2. When I look into the service quotation I can't see where can I find the ATP information : quantity confirmed, date confirmed...

3. I checked the service order in order then to compare with service quotation : and in the service order, the first step is to filter the nature of the service order item : Service part items, and then I am able to click on the button "Check availability" and find the information in the item tab. I have nothing similar in service quotation (no filter for item...), or no information in the items details page.

In the help portal I can't find any information about the ATP for service quotation. I can find informations only for service order ATP.

Can you explain me how does it work, please? Did I missed something ? wthat is the standard process proposed by SAP in this case (step by step) ? or is it in the road map ?

Thank you very much for your help.

PS : I also expect this functionnality in the repair in house quotation.

entry.title: Termination processes and when Offboarding can be triggered
entry.content:
When an employee is terminated, all the necessary information to terminate the contract will be sent to the payroll system, and any other stakeholders will be informed. Standard offboarding will be triggered immediately once the termination is approved in Employee Central. But what happens if the person who resigned is a talent? Typically, organizations try to retain talent, and if the negotiation is successful, HR needs to proceed with the manual cancellation of the termination event and offboarding. It's also necessary to check if the termination has already been replicated in the payroll system and to remove the termination of the contract. In this blog, written with @BarbaraSerio ,there is an alternative solution to use offboarding as a pre-termination process.

Step to configure

  • Create an event reason 'Pre-termination' as a data change event. This way, the employee status will remain active.

  • If an event derivation rule is configured in Employee Central, add the event reason to the rule based on customer requirements (for example, based on the value of a specific field in the Job Information portlet).

  • Configure the business rule for Offboarding adding this event reason 

  • Configure a specific program for the negotiation 

HR can start the process by adding the data change event and offboarding will be triggered. If the negotiation fails, then a termination can be added manually in Employee Central.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content:
@Cocquerel - Great question and Let me try best to explain the positioning briefly.

SAP Databricks & SAP AI Core serve distinct purposes in AI/ML and data processing.

  • SAP Databricks:

    • Offers a broader scope, allowing the blending and harmonization of SAP, non-SAP, structured, and unstructured data. Integrated and component of SAP BDC with zero-copy and delta share capabilities to consume SAP managed data products, BW data products, etc.
    • Supports end-to-end AI development, including data engineering, preparation, AI/ML development, and model lifecycle management.
    • Beneficial for integrating diverse data sources and leveraging advanced data science and AI/ML tools.

  • SAP AI Core:

    • Focuses on enabling customers to build custom AI solutions exclusively using SAP business applications data. Empowering your SAP solutions with integrated artificial intelligence capabilities.
    • Provides a platform for developing AI models leveraging rich SAP data.
    • Ideal for businesses prioritizing AI development within their existing SAP data infrastructure.

In essence, SAP AI Core manages and deploys AI models specifically within SAP applications, while SAP Databricks is a partnership that provides a platform for broader data integration and AI development using SAP data alongside other enterprise data.

Happy learning,
Jeet

entry.title: Credit Management: Same day Credit Recheck on Released Sales Order in SAP S/4Hana Public Cloud
entry.content:
Business requirement is Same day Credit Recheck on Released Sales Order in SAP S4 PERSON_NAME Public Cloud. 

We have checked SAP note DIGITS/DIGITS, but the solution does not work in S4 PERSON_NAME Public Cloud.

In ECC business can use VKM* transactions but same transactions are not available in S4 PERSON_NAME Public Cloud.

Need a solution from SAP to Credit Recheck on Released Sales Order on same day.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Credit Management: Same day Credit Recheck on Release...
entry.content: As per standard, a credit check will be triggered again for a released sales order if any changes are made to the order or at the item level.

entry.title: SAP S/4 Public cloud - Service - Service quotation/repair quotation - Copy from template
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I am implementing SAP S/4HANA public cloud service module for a customer. I have seen that we are able to create service order via the help of the service order template, by clicking on the "copy from template" button or I can create a service order with the button Create service order - Use template 

 

I can't see it in the service quotation or repair quotation. I'm really surprised because the real process flow start by the quotation that I send to my customer to give him the price of the service I am able to deliver. And the purpose of the service order template is really to help the businnes users to fulfill the item tab. I don't understand why the button are not shown.

 

Can you explain me how does it work, please? Did I missed something ? wthat is the standard process proposed by SAP in this case (step by step) ? or is it in the road map ? (I know that on premise, this fonctionnality already works for quotation).

Thank you very much for your help.

PS : I also expect this functionnality in the repair in house quotation.

entry.title: Re: Sort and Dynamic Sub-Total in Adobe Forms in S/4 HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi

To sort a table we can use the "instanceManager,moveInstance" property to reduce the code.

Example: Sorting by Material we can position the formcalc code at the "Material field" ready:form event

Assuming the Table row name will be "Row1"

tbl_Material::ready:form - (FormCalc, client)
var tbl = "$.Row1" // Table row
var i = 0 // Source row
var j = 0 // Compared row
var tblLength = xfa.resolveNode( tbl ).all.length // Number of rows
var source // Source concatenated fields to compare
var destination // Compare to concatenated fields
// *** Loop from the first row
for i = 0 upto tblLength - 1 do
  // *** Compare with the subsequence rows
  for j = i + 1 upto tblLength -1 do
    source       = xfa.resikveNode( tbl)[ i ].Material      // Concatenate fields source to compare
    destination = cfa.resolveNode( tbl )[ j ].Material    // Concatenate fields to compare with
    if ( source > destination ) then
      xfa.resolveNode( tbl).instanceManager.moveInstance( j, i )
    endif
  endfor // j
endfor // i

Regards

entry.title: Re: How do we migrate Open Accruals into a Condition Cont...
entry.content: Thanks Hari for the response. I am not able to make REBD post accruals without a business volume. Eg: If i want to migrate contracts from 1st March, i am putting start date as 28th Feb and maintaining REBD condition as say 10,000 and Rebate % as 3%. I expected the system to create an accrual for 300 on 28th Feb. However, it is coming as 0. It is natural that there are no business volumes on 28th Feb as the system will be live only from 1st March. Can you tell me whether what i am doing is correct and why i dont get accruals posted?

entry.title: Re: Event-Based Revenue Recognition for Service (Advanced Execution) - Time & Expense-based invo
entry.content:
Hi Alice nice information, thanks so much.

We need to know if we have some cost in PM order that we would not be billed but this cost  would take part of the result in event-revenued recognition for advanced execution.

For instance We plan in pm order for 2 hours for labour.

We bill one hour but the other hour takes part of the cost in the event-revenued recognition for advanced execution.

We don´t know what happends with the 1 hour that takes part of the cost of the pm order and should be costo also for service order.

Thanks so much for your collaboration

entry.title: Scheduling dates through developer extensibility
entry.content:
Intro

Available-to-Promise (ATP) is a business function in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. It provides a response to order fulfillment requests in Sales & Delivery and Production Planning. Scheduling functionality is a crucial part of ATP, offering this functioanlity as an API empower users to meet specific customer requirements concerning order entry and delivery-date calculations for Sales Order requirements.

 

API for ATP Scheduling

To schedule dates through developer extensibility we introduced in the release 2502 of S/4HANA Public Cloud the API for ATP Scheduling: CL_ATP_SCHEDULING_API. This API class is used to determine scheduling dates like Material availability date/time (MBDAT), Transportation planning date/time (TDDAT), Loading date/time (LDDAT), Goods issue date/time (WADAT), Delivery date/time (LFDAT) and Goods Receipt End Date (ELDAT). This API determines these dates based on provided inputs from customer implemented Business Add-In (BAdI), with the goal of calculating and adjusting the Requested Delivery Date (RDD) in the Sales Order.

If you, as a customer, want to use this API, you can do so for example by implementing the BAdI SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM_REQDATE as part of key user extensibility. This can be done in the App Custom Logic using the role SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR.

When the method EXECUTE_SCHEDULING of API CL_ATP_SCHEDULING_API is called with the necessary inputs within customer specific implementation of a BADI method IF_EX_SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM_REQDATE~MODIFY_ITEM_REQ_DAT, it returns scheduled dates and a message table which displays messages pertaining to failure or success of the API.

Example

Company XYZ receives sales orders from 2 regions and needs to adjust the RDD dynamically, on the basis of the warehouse processing time and the shipping duration. Region A is at a farther location than Region B. So, in that case, due to longer shipping duration, the sales orders from Region A should have an extra two days added for processing. Adding the 2 days to the RDD can be implemented in the BAdI.

The interesting part is to ensure validity of this newly calculated date. For this you can call the API (eg.: to schedule with Business Process Scheduling (BPS) within ATP) for validation of the new dates.

Here is an example implementation of the BAdI how you can call the API for validating and/or recalculating the RDD:

 

"Input date_time can be any timestamp in UTC format. The date and time should be converted if required.
    "Example how to get current date_time.
    GET TIME STAMP FIELD DATA(lv_date_time).

*    "Example implementation of how to convert RDD into the date_time using Ship-to Party time zone.
*    DATA(lv_shiptoparty) = salesdocumentpartners[ partnerfunction = 'WE' ]-customer.
*    SELECT SINGLE i_organizationaddress~addresstimezone FROM i_customer WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS INNER JOIN i_organizationaddress WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS
*      ON i_customer~addressid = i_organizationaddress~addressid
*      AND i_organizationaddress~addressrepresentationcode IS INITIAL
*      WHERE i_customer~customer = _shiptoparty
*      INTO (lv_shiptoparty_timezone).
*    CONVERT DATE salesdocument-requesteddeliverydate TIME '000000' INTO TIME STAMP DATA(lv_date_time) TIME ZONE lv_shiptoparty_timezone.

    cl_atp_scheduling_api=>get_instance( )->execute_scheduling(
      EXPORTING
        is_scheduling_param  = VALUE #( sales_document = salesdocument-salesdocument
                                        sales_document_item = salesdocumentitem-salesdocumentitem
                                        date_time = lv_date_time
                                        date_type = '50' )
      IMPORTING
        et_message               = DATA(lt_message)
        et_scheduling_result     = DATA(lt_scheduling_result)
    ).

    IF lt_scheduling_result IS NOT INITIAL.
      "Update RDD if necessary.
      requesteddeliverydate = lt_scheduling_result[ 1 ]-delivery_date.
    ENDIF.

 

Result

  • Initial Requirement: Requested Delivery Date eg. February 10, 2025 (Monday) – the date at the point of sales order creation.
  • Updated Requirement: The customer specific implementation of BAdI will call the API as shown above and retrieves the updated and scheduled delivery dates shifting it 2 days into the future to February 12, 2025 based on BPS settings.

 

Outro

The Scheduling API, is an important component used to manage scheduling dates in alignment with standard scheduling systems. The integration of this API within the customer specific implementation SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM_REQDATE BAdI makes sure that there is accuracy in the scheduling and adjustment of the RDD. This helps in maintaining consistency and ensuring that you are adhering to scheduling policies.

entry.title: Re: Customer returns to be posted directly to Quality wit...
entry.content:
Dear TMRajapaksha,

thank you for the details provided.

I guess you face error QA495 while you have an active inspection type 04 for the material and try to post the return.

It is normal, and the aim is to avoid stock inconsistencies. Please read SAP Note

824786 - QA495 despite deactivating the QM inspection.

When a stock-relevant inspection type is active in the material master, QM has exclusive control over the stock which is "enforced" via inspection lots. It is the standard process.

Have a nice day,
Albert

entry.title: Re: Hello Everyone,I want to add a tab in the additional...
entry.content: @Arthi2  you have not provided the screenshots

entry.title: VIP Virtual Program – Strategic Product Roadmap Update H1 2025 (APJ/EMEA)
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the option "Register Now" button or "Yes" to "Will you be attending?" will NOT register you for the event. To register for the event, please REGISTER HERE in order to receive the event invite and login details. 

 

Session Information:

Join us to hear about the Solution Roadmap and broader view on the strategy and the direction that SAP SuccessFactors is taking for 2025 and beyond.
New: Please note the session will have available closed captions as well as ZOOM automatic close caption translation. (Available languages: Spanish, Portuguese, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Thai, German, French and many more!)

 
APJ & EMEA Session - 2nd April, 2025
16:00 SGT Singapore
9:00 CET Europe

REGISTER HERE!

 

If you have any questions, please get in touch with your Regional Program Manager: 

For Americas karla.trevino@sap.com

For Europe, Middle East & Africa samanta.ferreri@sap.com

For Asia Pacific, Japan & Greater China vanesa.medricka@sap.com

entry.title: Re: Outbound delivery creation on STO with TM relevant pu...
entry.content: In VLB10 under tab "User Role" there is a checkbox "With Ext. TMS-Relevant Orders", if this checkbox is active, the STO appears in the list.

entry.content: Not sure if its late but let me know if you need volunteers. you can reach out to me at aruny2652@gmail.com

entry.title: Re: How to get User Id of Initiator in Mass Processing/Consolidation, Instead of SAP_WFRT?
entry.content: Thank you, we are facing similar issue. Will implement your solution.

entry.title: SAP PO REST receive multipart/form-data text-parameters
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

thank you for your time.

Technical Specs:

  • SAP PO 7.50 SP30

Scenario:

It is required to connect the SAP PO to a 3rd party system. This 3rd party System offers a REST API. Therefore, I would like to use the REST receiver channel.

The problem is, that the 3rd party system requires the REST request to have content type multipart/form-data. It requires a PDF File to be sent as an attachment and two form-data parameters to be provided. Here a screenshot from Postman:

Note that two of the for-data parameters are texts, whereas the third is a file.

When I route this postman request to webhook.site, this is the result:

Is it possible to replicate this functionality in a REST receiver channel?

I have tried using the module parameter MessageTransformBean to achieve this, but without success.

Thank you in advance!

Greetings,
Nick

entry.title: VIP Virtual Program – Strategic Product Roadmap Update H1 2025 (AMER/EMEA)
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the option "Register Now" button or "Yes" to "Will you be attending?" will NOT register you for the event. To register for the event, please REGISTER HERE in order to receive the event invite and login details. 

 

Session Information:

Join us to hear about the Solution Roadmap and broader view on the strategy and the direction that SAP SuccessFactors is taking for 2025 and beyond.

AMER & EMEA Session - April 1st 2025

11:00 EDT USA | Puerto Rico

entry.title: Re: Hello Everyone,I want to add a tab in the additional...
entry.content:
Before you paste your screen copies, this is a classic exit, so from CMOD you should :

  • In EXIT_SAPLCV110_001 provide title text for custom dynpro 1 (if initial dynpro wont be displayed)
  • Define required data for exchange between Abap memory and Dynpro in include ZXCV110TOP of exit function group XCV11
  • Create the subscreen with your fields and logic referencing data from the top include
  • In EXIT_SAPLCV110_002 PBO logic pass data from main program to structures defined in top include
  • In EXIT_SAPLCV110_003 PAI logic pass data from structures defined in top include to main program
  • In EXIT_SAPLCV110_004 handle function codes from main program dynpro
  • Activate dynpro, includes, project and test

entry.title: Re: Hide Matrix reports in display options
entry.content:
Hi @Uroojafz 

By default, it would show the options All Reports, Direct Reports only and the Matrix Reports only. As of this time there is no option to hide it as it is hard coded. 

If you think there should be an option to hide it. You can submit an enhancement request at our influence site.

 

Thanks,

Gerald

entry.title: Compare two clients (SAP environments) in SAP Signavio
entry.content:
@SAP Signavio Community:

Context: each time we acquire a company, we create a new environment in SAP.

Is it possible to compare two clients (SAP environments) in the same time in SAP Signavio (Insights, KPIs, analysis, diagrams)? 

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: How to include Project System in Make-to-Stock proces...
entry.content:
Normally, Projects Systems module can be used for Service Industry or if the company have multiple internal projects and they want to capture all internal costs depending upon the projects, then, PS will come into picture.
Now coming to your query, if you are a PS consultant, you need to bifurcate the Organizational Structure into

  • Project Definition
  • WBS Elements
  • Networks
  • Activities which should further to capture internal and external activities

Again I doubt, for Sugar Industry with Make-To-Stock process, PS is not required.

entry.title: Re: Inspection Lot XXXX for order XXXX does not have nece...
entry.content:
Hi spartan0412,

thank you for the details provided.

It worth to check also, whether the - still active - long-term inspection (status LTIN instead of ICCO) is causing the block for the order closure:

499507 - CO440 when setting the deletion flag in the order

Have a nice day,
Albert

entry.title: Re: [SAC] Value with asterisk shown on year and quarter s...
entry.content: It could be that you need a currency filter for them to show correctly.

entry.title: Re: WebI: Working with Excel-Files as datasource
entry.content:
Hello, I know this is quite an old post, however I am suddenly having trouble with a report that has 2 data providers from SAP and an Excel spreadsheet.  It has worked for years, but now I am getting this error.  Its a pretty generic one. It happens when I choose run and it says refreshing the document. Instead of bringing up the filters, I get the error below. I have changed spreadsheets etc and nothing seems to work.

We are using SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform 4.3 Support Pack 3 Patch 7 Version : 14.3.3.4727.7

 

Error

 
An internal error occurred while calling 'processDPCommandsEx' API. (Error: ERR_WIS_30270)

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Challenge March - SAP Community Escape House - AI 🤖🧠
entry.content: Totally agree @Cocquerel! I also wanted it to be more difficult but as it is a new topic to many people we decided to make it easier for this one. Next time 😉

entry.title: Re: SAP SIGNAVIO CAN´T CHANGE CURRENCY
entry.content:
Good day XimenaReina,

Thank you for sharing your question on the SAP Community.  To provide support in regard to your question, 

Please create a support case using SAP for Me (log in with S-User ID and password).  

You will need to select the specific SAP Signavio product / component as shown in the example below.

System:  
Search for SAP Signavio   

 

Product Area:  
SAP Signavio Process Manager > SAP Signavio Process Manager  

 

Component:  
BPI (Business Process Intelligence)  

---  
BPI-PI (SAP Signavio Process Insights) or  

BPI-SIG (SAP Business Transformation Suite by SAP Signavio) – Product & Topic  

For further information you can find more details in the SAP Signavio Onboarding Resource Center .

 

Kind regards,

KantriceD

entry.title: Re: How to create additional 89 inspection lot during goo...
entry.content:
Dear pablon86,

thank you for the details provided.

I recommend to read KBA:

3573211 - Is it possible to create inspection lot with function module / BAPI / API?

There is a way (API) to create 89 lot automatically:

https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_INSPECTIONLOT_SRV_0001/path/post_A_InspectionLot

Have a nice day,
Albert

entry.content: Read this KBA 2656789 - Error CO440 while closing production order/process order (read the error message long text if you don't have a S-id)

entry.content: Sorry for the confusion (My IQ really showed here), but thanks that helped!

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content:
The "Achilles' heel" of Datasphere is the extraction from REST / SOAP web services, currently only the OData as REST web source is integrated (via remote table with SDA or via Dataflows).

Having a Python environment inside SAP Databricks could solve these issues.

Does SAP Databricks have data extraction capabilities for web service data sources?

entry.content: Hello did you find the error?

Hope to feel that hardcore experience (ABAP, CASP and the hidden ones)...

Maybe a challenging integration with Python or another SDK's

entry.title: Public Cloud - Manage Automatic Payment for Payment Method H
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I have the following problem. I am trying to run a payment for a customer with payment method H (bill of exchange) via “Manage automatic payments”, but I get the following error message:
For incoming bill of exchange payment method H variant of report RFWEBU00 named DISKONTIERUNG, INKASSO or FORFAITIERUNG must be defined dependent on bill of exchange usage.

Unfortunately, I can't find any information on how to maintain the variants. Do you have any ideas? I would be very grateful for your help.

Thanks, Güli

entry.title: SAP project system
entry.content:
SAP project system in cj20n screen in network activity person assignment tab is getting warning message person not found. Can we make as error message so user can input correct employee name in person assignment tab. please help me with solution how can I achieve this issue with details steps

Message class - CY

Message no - 376

entry.title: BAPI
entry.content:
Hi All,

Im trying to build a program same as qm01,the only difficulty im facing is im not able to update fields like reference quantity ,sales office ,sales group through BAPI BAPI_QUALNOT_GETDETAIL ,is there any other way to do it.
Thanks.

I did this already but i can not see the link button. i sent an email to SAP but i was told the email i am trying to link to my account doesnt have a user. so i do not understand. i need help

entry.title: Firebase integration add documents vs set documents
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I hope I'm in the right group; I searched unsuccessfully for a solution to add documents via Configure Firestore data entity, document id is not possible to assign, in the google firebase firestore documentation it says to use set and not add to assign a non-automatic id but for example userId, can someone help me understand if it's a limitation or if I can't find the solution? forgive my bad English

I can update or create all the other fields but not document

entry.title: Re: Unable to close project / WBS
entry.content: What shows up when you run CJI5? Try running CJEN too.

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to get ABAP Usage data like SCMON for...
entry.content:
Hi, Hrishikesh_Mangi,
I am not familiar with SCMON / UPL, so I'm not sure how to answer your question.

Cloud ALM does document what "IS" supported for all systems/services at:
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/supported-solutions
You can filter the SAP Solution by SAP BTP, ABAP environment and get to all capabilities that are available for monitoring purposes.

I hope this is helpful to you.

Best regards,
Paula

entry.content: Its good! 🙂 How about user story - requirement? Can we assign a user story to multiple requirements?

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba 2502 Release Key Innovations Preview
entry.content: Existing customers cannot migrate their data to the new data center.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Planning Copy from Upload Version to Public Version: Overwrite issue
entry.content:
Hello, 

We are trying to copy data from a private version, where the data is uploaded using a flat file with "Data Uploader", the requirement is to overwrite the data. But when we copy it using data actions, first time it works fine as there is no data in the public version. With the second upload (the upload version is completely emptied before the upload), the data for the missing dimensions is set to 0.00 instead of just overwriting what is in the second upload:

Example:

  • In first Upload and Copy to Public Works fine (as Period 01-04 and Dimensions L010 and L030 have similar data in Version _UPLOAD and in the public version EP2-2024

  • In second Upload it can be seen the Data for L030 (Period 1)  and L010 (Period 4) is set to 0 although it should have ignored it. All other dimensions are correctly "overwritten" as per settings in data action.

     

    We tried with normal copy data action with overwrite as well as with Advanced formulas but with no success. Did anyone face similar issue or does anyone know how it can solved? Thank You!

entry.title: Re: BAPI
entry.content:
For missing fields, you can be required to use BAdI NOTIF_EVENT_SAVE as suggested in 1667389 - The BAPI_QUALNOT_CREATE does not include the field MALFUNCTION DATE. As there are no EXTENSIONIN parameter, you could save the required data in memory in driver program then read and process it in the BAdI, then free the memory.

Program could look like

  • BAPI_QUALNOT_GETDETAIL
  • EXPORT <missing parameters> TO MEMORY ID id 
  • BAPI_QUALNOT_CREATE
  • BAPI_QUALNOT_SAVE
  • BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT or ROLLBACK
  • FREE MEMORY ID id

entry.title: Re: From Application Logging to Cloud Logging: Service Innovation Guide
entry.content:
@mohit_bansal3 would you please explain what do you mean by custom metrics ? its all about ingest URL afaik . Cloud Foundry just streams all the logs into the ingest URL , be the instance created directly in your sub account or shared from another sub account - what am I missing ? 

also can you paste the log drain URL you use, removing the credentials from it ? 

 

@yyertuganov can we see the mta.yaml , details of UPS ( screenshot or so ). ?

entry.title: Re: Comprehensive Guide to Unit Testing in CAP
entry.content: @VishalKwatra I achieved what I was looking for , thank you .

entry.title: Re: Update the dimension member in a transactional record...
entry.content:
@saikhancs35 

Not sure but I think you have over simplified your example. From your example it just looks like a copy from # to MD01 for Dim A. You could just use a copy data action to do so and specify in the rule. You can delete the # data after copy if required.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Ratings on Compensation Worksheet appearing in Decima...
entry.content:
Hi @bankarmanokam 

Please find below the reply from our product team :

Rating type should not be default in comp. If rating is pulled from overall competency section then comp configuration should point to it specifically.

Let me know if it fixes the issue after you change default and then relaunch worksheets.

Thanks

Xavier

entry.title: AWS Mail configuration for SAP ABAP Systems
entry.content:
Introduction:-

If you have moved your SAP system to AWS, you may want to eliminate some mail servers that are still operating on-premises or avoid the deployment of an EC2 instance dedicated to running an SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) server. Sending outgoing emails is one of the most common requirements when running a SAP system. These emails can come from a variety of sources, including workflows, batch processing and process chains, monitoring and alerting etc.

Amazon Simple Email Service (SES)

Customers can send emails from within any application with Amazon Simple Email Service (SES), which is affordable, flexible, and scalable. You can quickly configure Amazon SES to support a variety of email use cases, such as transactional, marketing, or mass email communications. In this blog, we'll walk you through the necessary steps to set up outbound mail from a SAP ABAP system using Amazon SES (Simple Email Service) service. 

Prerequisites:-

In order to do preliminary testing, we will first set up a "Sandbox" account in Amazon SES and confirm a sender email address.  All emails originating from our SAP systems will be accepted by the SES service once all setup procedures have been completed successfully and this account has been converted to production.

Go to Amazon SES in the AWS EC2 Console, choose Email Addresses, and then click "Verify a New Email Address."  Please check your mail inbox after entering your email address and clicking Verify This Email Address.

After the validation is completed, the Status will change to green

Once the email address verification is completed, we need to create proper SMTP credentials which will be used by our SAP systems. To create the credentials, click on “SMTP Settings” and press the “Create My SMTP Credentials” button.

Note the Server Name as it will be required afterwards during the SAP system configuration

Enter a username and click on create bottom in the bottom

Download the SMTP username and password credentials in a csv file

SAP ABAP Outbound Email Configuration:-

Go to ABAP system and call transaction SCOT, select the SMTP nodes, and create a new one via the wizard

Click on the settings button, and provide the credentials generated in the previous steps

Download the certificate from the Amazon SES SMTP endpoint (for example using this cmd line on a Linux system : openssl s_client -starttls smtp -connect email-smtp.us-east-1.amazonaws.com:587 | sed -ne ‘/-BEGIN CERTIFICATE-/,/-END CERTIFICATE-/p’)

Note: the Amazon SES endpoints are region dependent, so please ensure you are using the one within the same region.

In transaction STRUST, import the certificate into the SSL client (Standard) and restart the service via SMICM

Check outbound email:-

In transaction SO01, create a test email and press the send button

In transaction SOST, We can trigger a send job via menu Send Request à Start Send Process for Selection.

Conclusion:-

In this blog, we have demonstrated how to set up SAP ABAP systems to send out emails, which is a frequent requirement from the standpoints of business processes and base operations.

Related information:-

https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/awsforsap/category/sap/ 

https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/awsforsap/simplify-sap-jobs-scheduling-using-aws-native-tooling/ 

Please share your feedback and your thoughts. Write back to me for questions and queries.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: AWS Mail configuration for SAP ABAP Systems
entry.content: Thanks for sharing valuable content.

entry.title: Re: Become an SAP Certified Development Associate - SAP Integration Suite: New Certification out no
entry.content: This blog post is now outdated. For the most up to date information about the SAP Integration Suite certification and learning resources to help you prepare, check out this blog post.

entry.title: Data import of Local Currency only in the measure based model in SAC Analytical Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Folks,

Heed your expertise with the below query in new measure based model.

Requirement: Users have to collect the Actuals data with both Local Currency and Group Currency  and Plan data is to be collected at Local Currency and that gets converted to Group Currency using conversion mechanism in the SAC

Model defined: Owing to the given requirement , We have created 2 Measure in the Model(New Model) Called LC and GC. We are able to upload the LC and GC in the actual Version, But when we tried to load the plan data containing only LC value through flat file, it is asking us to even map the GC measure and due to which it is throwing an error called" This model required all the measure to be mapped" not allowing us to load the data.

 

Query:

Is this behavior is by design or it is something influence due to some setting?

If this is by design, then SAC restricting the users to load the selected measure (Either LC or GC or Quantity) making an restricting on the process normally allowing different users to load different type of measure.

 

 

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP Community
entry.content: Welcome to the SAP Community, it is great to have you here! 🙂
